completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/test_supervisor_with_tools.py creates test_config + 4-entry capability_registry fixtures; calls build_app_state(test_config).
completion_promises.evidence: Created tests/test_supervisor_with_tools.py with stub_registry_config fixture (a JarvisConfig variant of test_config pinning stub_capabilities_path to the canonical 4-entry stub yaml) and capability_registry fixture (loads the 4-entry list via load_stub_registry; asserts len==4). Tests in TestAC001NineToolWiring, TestAC003CapabilityBlockInjection, and TestAC005NoLLMCallNoNetwork all invoke build_app_state(stub_registry_config) inside the network-isolated patch context.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Asserts the compiled supervisor graph exposes exactly the 9 tool names in alphabetical order: calculate, capabilities_refresh, capabilities_subscribe_updates, dispatch_by_capability, get_calendar_events, list_available_capabilities, queue_build, read_file, search_web.
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC001NineToolWiring contains three checks: (1) test_compiled_supervisor_exposes_nine_tool_names walks the compiled CompiledStateGraph via _extract_tool_names and asserts every one of the 9 expected names is present (uses the same introspection helper as test_supervisor_lifecycle_wiring.py); (2) test_create_deep_agent_receives_nine_tools_alphabetically wraps create_deep_agent with Mock(wraps=...) and asserts kwargs['tools'] yields exactly EXPECTED_TOOL_NAMES in alphabetical order; (3) test_assemble_tool_list_is_alphabetical pins the assemble_tool_list contract directly. All three pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Asserts the rendered system prompt contains the {available_capabilities} block built from 4 descriptors (each as_prompt_block() substring appears verbatim).
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC003CapabilityBlockInjection.test_each_descriptor_block_appears_verbatim_in_system_prompt wraps create_deep_agent with Mock(wraps=...), runs build_app_state, captures the system_prompt kwarg, then asserts: (a) no raw {available_capabilities}/{date}/{domain_prompt} placeholders survived format(); (b) for each of the 4 descriptors, descriptor.as_prompt_block() appears as a substring in the system prompt verbatim. The companion test_blocks_appear_in_alphabetical_agent_id_order also asserts the blocks render in agent_id-sorted order. Both tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Asserts empty-registry path: build_supervisor(test_config, tools=[], available_capabilities=[]) renders the 'No capabilities currently registered.' sentinel.
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC004EmptyRegistryFallback.test_empty_registry_renders_sentinel calls build_supervisor(test_config, tools=[], available_capabilities=[]) with create_deep_agent wrapped via Mock(wraps=...); inspects kwargs['system_prompt'] and asserts the literal sentinel 'No capabilities currently registered.' is present. Also asserts the result is a CompiledStateGraph and that no descriptor-style header (### …Tools:) leaked through ahead of the sentinel.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No LLM call is made (FakeListChatModel or equivalent); no network.
completion_promises.evidence: Every test in this file patches jarvis.agents.supervisor.init_chat_model to return the conftest FakeListChatModel fixture (fake_llm) — no real provider client is constructed. TestAC005NoLLMCallNoNetwork.test_fake_llm_response_cursor_remains_at_zero pins the no-invocation invariant by asserting FakeListChatModel.i (the response cursor that increments on every call) stays at 0 after build_app_state, and verifies init_chat_model was called exactly once with config.supervisor_model. (Note: I cannot use Mock(wraps=fake_llm) because DeepAgents' resolve_model uses isinstance(model, BaseChatModel) for short-circuit — see comment block in the test class.)
